steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content:
The mass decreases by $18\%$ every $9.42$ minutes, so the percent remaining each period is
$$100\%-18\%=82\%$$
That gives a decay factor of $0.82$.

steps.title: Count elapsed periods
steps.content:
Since each decay period lasts $9.42$ minutes, the number of periods after $t$ minutes is
$$\frac{t}{9.42}$$
So the model must use base $0.82$ and exponent $\frac{t}{9.42}$.

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content:
The equation with that base and exponent is
$$M=120(0.82)^{\frac{t}{9.42}}$$
The answer is A.
